title: Pilostyles pringlei
taxonomy: Pilostyles pringlei is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is an obligate root parasite, meaning it relies on the roots of other plants to obtain the necessary nutrients for its survival. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Pilostyles pringlei is closely related to other plants such as periwinkle and mandrake. Further classification places it within the genus Pilostyles, which is comprised of several other species of root-parasitic plants.
